keyPoints: Introduction to Queue: A Queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure where the element added first will be removed first.
Queue Operations: enqueue(adding element), dequeue(removing element), peek(looking at front element), isEmpty(checking if queue is empty)
Queue Implementation using Arrays: Using arrays to implement a queue can be done by using two indices, one for the front and one for the rear.
Circular Queues: A circular queue is a type of queue where the last element is connected to the first element, forming a circle.
Double-Ended Queues: A double-ended queue is a type of queue where elements can be added or removed from both ends.
Priority Queue: A priority queue is a type of queue where elements are ordered based on their priority, with the highest priority element being removed first.
